content.problemStatement:
We have NNN intervals I1,I2,...,INI_1, I_2, ..., I_NI1‚Äč,I2‚Äč,...,IN‚Äč where Ij=[Lj,Rj]I_j = [L_j, R_j]Ij‚Äč=[Lj‚Äč,Rj‚Äč] meaning it contains all real numbers between LjL_jLj‚Äč and RjR_jRj‚Äč. It is guaranteed that all 2‚čÖN2 \cdot N2‚čÖN endpoints of the intervals are distinct.

Construct a graph of NNN nodes such that there is an edge between nodes XXX and YYY if and only if the following 2 conditions are **both** satisfied :


There exists at least one real number contained in IXI_XIX‚Äč but not in IYI_YIY‚Äč
There exists at least one real number contained in IYI_YIY‚Äč but not in IXI_XIX‚Äč
Find the maximum size of a clique‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† in this constructed graph.

‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† Maximum size of a clique refers to the Clique Problem


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the number of intervals.
The next NNN lines contain 222 integers each - LjL_jLj‚Äč and RjR_jRj‚Äč, the left and right endpoints of the interval.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum clique size of the constructed graph
constraints: All the 2‚čÖN2 \cdot N2‚čÖN points LjL_jLj‚Äč and RjR_jRj‚Äč are distinct
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1 :
The edges present in the graph are (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) and (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) but the edge (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) is not present.

Edge (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) is present because

1.51.51.5 is a real number belonging to I1I_1I1‚Äč but not I3I_3I3‚Äč
5.715.715.71 is a real number belonging to I3I_3I3‚Äč but not I1I_1I1‚Äč.


Edge (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) is not present because

There does not exist a real number belonging to I2I_2I2‚Äč but not I1I_1I1‚Äč.



The maximum clique size in this graph is formed by taking the subset (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3) or the subset (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) both of which have size 222.
